It comes with a customizable workout planner, an extensive exercise library, and a members-only Facebook group page. You can choose new workouts and track your progress with the app so that you can see how close you are to your fitness goals.<br/><br/>Don't forget to join the Jefit community so that you can be a part of it too!<br/><br/>We are currently living in very turbulent times, and now more than ever, the benefits of choosing the best home gym equipment goes beyond increasing just strength. We are hearing, thinking, and reading more and more about the growing COVID-19 outbreak. It looks like living with the coronavirus will be the new norm for a while and it's enough to raise anyone's stress level. A continued, elevated stress level, coupled with no exercise could potentially suppress your immune system.<br/><br/>Benefits of Having a Home Gym<br/><br/>One of the best and safest options you have for getting in great shape is doing it right from the comfort of your own home or apartment. Not to mention, this seems to be a no-brainer with COVID-19 hanging around for months to come. In addition to the many health & safety benefits, there is also an associated cost savings resulting from your gym temporarily closing.<br/><br/>Cost Savings Benefit<br/><br/>You do not have to spend a ton of money setting up your home gym. There are many different pieces of equipment you could use but it will depend first on budget and then on availability of space and of course training experience. Let's assume that space, budget and experience are somewhat limited.<br/><br/>Exercise Equipment Needs<br/><br/>The following lists include multiple options for exercise products that are ideal for home use and can be purchased online. Each list offers versatile equipment, and is practical in terms of staying within a budget.<br/><br/>You can take care of your equipment needs while the Jefit team can help in terms of finding the best strength-training routines to help you get started.<br/><br/>Home Gym Option #1<br/><br/>Stability Ball<br/><br/>This product reaches far beyond just sitting on it for basic core work. Try lying in a supine position with your feet on the ball. Engage your core and raise your buttock off the floor, pause, and return to your starting position. Following a set of hip extension, perform the same movement with your feet on the ball and knees bent. Lift your buttock off the floor and hold in a bridge position, slowly lower and repeat. After completing these two movements, start in the same position but this time pull both feet in towards your buttock, performing a leg curl movement.<br/><br/>Foam Roller<br/><br/>If you're not rolling out on a regular basis, then you should honestly start. There are many benefits of adding in a few minutes of foam rolling before and even after your exercise session. Maintaining healthy fascia should be top of mind for you. Remember, restriction (i.e., connective/muscle tissue) is associated with disfunction and this can lead to a limited range of motion; and yes, this will eventually effect your performance.<br/><br/>Exercise Bands<br/><br/>Regular band work can be ideal for any exercise level. Bands are great to utilize in circuit-type exercise routines or as part of your dynamic warm-up. Different colored bands typically equate with different intensity levels. These are very inexpensive to purchase and can be brought anywhere for an exercise session.<br/><br/>Medicine Ball<br/><br/>This is another great tool for your exercise toolbox, if used correctly. It's ideal for adding resistance to various moves like squats, lunges or a step-up with a shoulder press. In the hands of the right person, a medicine ball can be a welcome addition to any home gym.<br/><br/>Jump Rope<br/><br/>One of the best forms of cardio exercise you can do is jumping rope. It's great for elevating your heart rate and easy to transition to – so it's ideal for any circuit-type or HIIT training program at home. Below is an example of how the JeFit app can help you keep track of this and many other exercises that you'll now be doing from home.<br/><br/>Home Gym Option #2<br/><br/>Adjustable Bench<br/><br/>This is a versatile piece of equipment for any gym. This multi-purpose bench will allow you to do many different upper body exercises and can even double as a "plyo-box" for steps-ups etc.<br/><br/>Dumbbells<br/><br/>A must for any home gym. Dumbbells, take up minimal space. A good pair of dumbbells can be used for just about any upper/lower body strength exercise. Start by purchasing a few pairs of dumbbells and build up to a full set over time.<br/><br/>Kettlebells<br/><br/>One of the many great things about this product is its wide scope of usage. You can use kettlebells for basic, traditional exercises to more advanced movements like a Carry exercise or a Turkish Get-up.<br/><br/>Ab-Roller<br/><br/>Another unique product that really targets your core and is great for developing core strength. It's one of those exercises that can really overload your core area so start slow and gradually build your repetitions and/or hold time.<br/><br/>TRX System<br/><br/>This training-system has become well-known and a staple used in everything from boot camp classes to home gyms. One of the best exercises you can do, in my opinion is an inverted row. If you ever want to test your strength level try completing 10 repetitions using strict form with a full range of motion.<br/><br/>Ever get to the gym, but find that you're just too tired or not bothered to actually work out? It's a completely normal feeling and there could be several reasons behind it. Here, we list why you may be feeling this way and recommend some solution to try and get your gym motivation back.<br/><br/>How to Get Your Gym Motivation Back<br/><br/>1. Get some sleep<br/><br/>Sleep deprivation can actually affect us much more than people think. If you've had a bad night or nights, then it can really impact your performance, as well as your mindset. Feeling tired and suffering from lack of sleep will really demotivate you.<br/><br/>Try to set a bedtime routine that you stick to. Go to sleep 7-9 hours before you have to wake up so that you can ensure you get enough sleep. Even if you may struggle at first, eventually, your body clock will remember and you'll be able to follow your sleep schedule.<br/><br/>2. Take a few days off<br/><br/>Yes, spending too much time at the gym can be a potential reason why you've lost motivation. When you train, you're constantly putting your body under stress. This is why rest days are important—this is when your body recovers. If you don't give yourself rest days, then your body doesn't have the time to recover properly. Sooner or later, you'll start to feel it.<br/><br/>So if you get to the gym and don't feel like working out, then take some time off. A day or two, or even more will help you take the time you need to recuperate. See how you feel after taking some gym time off.<br/><br/>3. Change your routine<br/><br/>Most of us tend to keep to the same workouts that we know. We stick to what is familiar and what we know works. Eventually, we become bored. If you're feeling unmotivated to go the gym, it could be because your fitness routine has become stale and stagnant. Doing the same workout over and over again will no longer challenge you. Without stimulation, we lose our motivation.<br/><br/>Try mixing up your training. Jefit has plenty of exercises that you can choose from to make a new workout plan and keep things exciting. Putting together something new and renew your motivation and get your gym mojo back.<br/><br/>If you want a change from the gym, then you can even try heading outside for a run or do some bodyweight exercises in the park. Join a new fitness class that you haven't tried before like boxing or cycling. A change of scenery might do you some good as well.<br/><br/>4. Define your goal<br/><br/>One of the biggest mistakes that people make is not having a fitness goal. Whether it's about losing weight, gaining muscle, hitting a new squat PR, or being able to run a half marathon in a certain time, having something to work towards makes the biggest difference in the gym.<br/><br/>5. Track your progress<br/><br/>Another reason why you may be feeling burnt out for the gym is that you don't see the progress you're making. If you're not tracking your progress, recording your workouts, taking progress pictures, then you won't be able to see how far you've come. It can be easy to fall into the rut of thinking that you haven't made a difference or that things have changed. This is why it's important to keep track.<br/><br/>Nothing can renew your motivation to get back into the gym than seeing where you were before and where you are now. It can help you picture where you will be in the future. Use a journal or a workout app (like Jefit!) that makes it easy for you to log everything and help get your gym motivation back.<br/><br/>6. Make it a habit<br/><br/>This may sound counterintuitive but you need more than motivation. Motivation is temporary and as you probably know, it comes and goes. To make sure that you keep going to the gym, even when you have no motivation, you need to make it a habit.<br/><br/>Set a fitness routine that you stick to, whether it's going to the gym before work or after work, or even during your lunch breaks. It can be difficult to start but if you keep it up, over time, it will become an integral part of your routine. Then you'll find yourself going to the gym even if you don't want to—because it's a habit. It's a great way to get your gym motivation back.<br/><br/>While you can try your best to avoid injuries, unfortunately, most people will experience them once in a while. If you are injured, then it's best to see a physical therapist or professional that can properly treat it. However, there are some common gym injuries, and here are some solutions to treat them.<br/><br/>How to Treat These Common Gym Injuries<br/><br/>1. Muscle Pulls and Strains<br/><br/>Other common gym injuries include pulling or straining your muscle. This is when a muscle is torn or overstretched. While this can occur anywhere on your body, the most common places are straining your hamstring, or neck and back.<br/><br/>It can occur due to overexertion, being overstretched, and not warming up properly.<br/><br/>Pulling or straining your muscle will limit your mobility and can cause pain once you hit a certain threshold. You may also experience stiffness, swelling or weakness. It can also range from mild to severe.<br/><br/>How to treat it<br/><br/>Depending on how severe it is, there are different ways to treat it. Resting the affected muscle is paramount. It can be tempting to "work through the pain", however, this can make it worse. So take a couple of days off before slowly starting to incorporate movement with the muscle. Bear in mind though, too much rest can also cause stiffness so you don't want to keep it immobile for long. Try to find a good balance.<br/><br/>When you do start using it again, don't push it too much. Overdoing it can exacerbate it.<br/><br/>How to prevent it<br/><br/>Warming up is crucial in preventing these common gym injuries. You need to properly prepare your body for your training session instead of jumping straight in. If your muscles aren't warm, then you risk tearing it.<br/><br/>A good warmup should be specific. For example, if you're planning on squatting, then do some air squats to mimic the same movement that you'll be doing, just without the weight. It may seem tedious but taking the time to warm up can really improve your athletic performance, while also prevent muscle strains and pulls.<br/><br/>Listening to your body and knowing the difference between pain and good pain is important. Good pain is when you're challenging yourself but not going over your threshold. The bad pain that you don't want is when you're hurting yourself to the point where you can potentially pull or strain a muscle.<br/><br/>2. Runner's Knee<br/><br/>A common gym injury is Runner's Knee or patellofemoral pain syndrome. This is when you feel pain or soreness around the kneecaps or have trouble sitting, standing, walking. The pain may be exacerbated when you try to walk downwards as well.<br/><br/>It occurs when the kneecap (patella) is misaligned. Weak or tight thigh muscles and overuse of the knee can also cause it.<br/><br/>Despite the name, runner's are not the only people who can experience this, although it is prevalent among them because running places much demand on the knees. Any other exercise that requires a lot of use of the knee can cause it.<br/><br/>How to treat it<br/><br/>If you feel pain in and around the knee then the first thing that you can do is rest it. Take 3-4 days of training off. If you do exercise, then try to avoid training that involves the knee such as lunging and squatting.<br/><br/>Another good idea is to ice it. Icing it will assist in reducing any swelling.<br/><br/>How to prevent it<br/><br/>Find a good pair of shoes that can offer really great support. This will help reduce the demand on your knee so that you can decrease the risk of getting runner's knee again. Arch supports will also help with this as well.<br/><br/>Incorporate strengthening exercises into your fitness routine for your knee. Work on your lower body such as your quads, lower back, hips, and abs. This can strengthen the areas around the knee and reduce the stress placed on them. Try the plank and glute bridges.<br/><br/>3. Sprained Wrist<br/><br/>The wrist is an easy area to overload and put too much pressure on. Because it is used in a variety of exercises and takes a lot of weight, wrist strain is a common gym injury.<br/><br/>There may be swelling and tenderness. It'll also hurt to put pressure on your wrists.<br/><br/>You can get a sprained wrist through repetitive movements that can cause chronic wrist strain. On the other hand, acute wrist strain is when it occurs suddenly such as bending the wrist past the normal threshold.<br/><br/>How to treat it<br/><br/>Ice your wrist to reduce swelling. Also, make sure you rest it. Adding more pressure to it will only make it worse. This means that you should avoid any exercises that involve putting stress on or bending the wrist.<br/><br/>How to prevent it<br/><br/>If you're prone to wrist strains, then try modified versions of your favorite exercises. Front squat by crossing your arms across your chest instead of using your wrists. Push-ups can also be done by folding your hands into fists so that your wrists remain straight instead of bent.<br/><br/>Wearing a wrist strap can also really assists in taking the pressure off the wrist.<br/><br/>See a professional<br/><br/>If you are experiencing one of these common gym injuries do not improve, then see a professional physiotherapist or doctor. Your physio can properly examine you and provide specific solutions to your needs.<br/><br/>Workout with Jefit<br/><br/>Want a workout app that can recommend some great exercises, help you schedule your workouts, and offers a supportive online community? Jefit is an app that can do all those things and more. It comes with an extensive exercise library, customizable workout planner, as well as a members-only Facebook group where you can connect and interact with your fellow Jefit members. | När du vill öka muskelstorlek och bygga styrka, bör du fokusera på att använda mer sammansatta styrkeövningar. Forskning över tiden har visat sammansatta övningar är överlägsna jämfört med andra typer av träning. Faktum är att en 2017-studie publicerad i Frontiers in Physiology tittade på träningsämnen som använde sammansatta kontra isoleringsövningar under en åttaveckorsperiod. Studien visade att gruppen som fokuserade på sammansatta styrkeövningar hade större vinster i både styrka och VO2 max. En andra studie publicerad 2019 stöder också användningen av multi-joint (MJ) över enkel-joint (SJ) övningar när man vill förbättra styrkan i det här fallet, i underkroppen. Forskarna rapporterade signifikanta styrkaökningar i både SJ och MJ-grupper, men MJ-gruppen såg signifikant större ökningar i 1-RM för alla benövningar som testades i studien.<br/><br/>Vad är sammansatta styrkeövningar?<br/><br/>Sammansatta övningar är multi-joint rörelser som arbetar flera muskler eller muskelgrupper på en gång (ACSM). Ett exempel skulle vara en Barbell Squat som fungerar många muskelgrupper som kärnan, ben, höfter och rygg. Ett annat exempel skulle vara en bänkpress övning som fungerar musklerna som utgör bröstet, axlar och armar. Sammansatta styrkeövningar är en stapelvara i många träningsprogram eftersom de är idealiska för att bygga styrka och lägga till storlek. Dessutom kommer en sammansatt övning att rekrytera mer muskelfiber och i sin tur bränna mer kalorier per minut än en enkel gemensam eller isoleringsövning. Sammansatta övningar kan utföras med hjälp av kroppsvikt, träningsband, hantlar eller ditt bästa alternativ en skivstång. Detta beror på att den genomsnittliga gym-goer kan lyfta 20% mer vikt med en skivstång jämfört med hantlar. Sammansatt motion är också viktigt eftersom de efterliknar aktiviteter i det dagliga livet (ADL).<br/><br/>Ett exempel på en sammansatt (eller multi-joint) övning: Pull-up<br/><br/>Exempel på Compound & Isolation Typ övningar<br/><br/>Sammansatta (Multi-joint) övningar<br/><br/>Isolering (enkel-joint) övningar<br/><br/>Squat<br/><br/>Benförlängning<br/><br/>Deadlift<br/><br/>Ben Curl<br/><br/>Dips<br/><br/>Tricep-förlängning<br/><br/>Militär press<br/><br/>Sido lateral höjning<br/><br/>Pull-Ups<br/><br/>Bicep Curl<br/><br/>Bänkpress<br/><br/>Hantel Bröst Fly<br/><br/>Vad är isoleringsövningar?<br/><br/>Isoleringsövningar fungerar bara en muskel eller muskelgrupp och bara en led i taget (ACSM). Exempel på isoleringsövningar inkluderar Biceps Curl eller en benförlängningsövning.<br/><br/>Kombinera både mult-joint barbell och enkel-joint hantel övningar i ett träningspass har visat sig fungera bra. Denna typ av kombination kan ses i den nya Jefit program, Compound Strength Rutin. Många maskinbaserade styrketräningsprodukter är utformade med isoleringsövningar i åtanke. Viss forskning har dock visat att en isolering eller enkel gemensam övning, som en bicepscurl, kan öka muskelhypertrofi mer än en multi-joint övning.<br/><br/>Ett exempel på en isolering (eller enkel-led) övning: Bicep Curl<br/><br/>Jefits nya rutin för styrka<br/><br/>Ett nytt avancerat styrkeprogram utformat kring multi-joint övningar är Jefit Compound Strength Routine. Det 3-dagars, avancerade styrketräningsprogrammet innehåller 9-10 styrkeövningar i varje träningspass. Rutinen erbjuder tre olika styrkeprogram, med hjälp av skivstång och hantlar, och inkluderar 1-3 supersets i varje träningspass. Denna typ av programdesign gör för en snabbare träning och håller i sin tur alla sessionstider mindre än en timme.<br/><br/>För att avsluta saker, läs den här bra listan med riktlinjer från styrkeexpert, Charles Poliquin, som diskuterar pro / con för att använda både sammansatta och isoleringsövningar. Det är definitivt värt att läsa. Var väl och håll dig stark!<br/><br/>Vi vet alla att motion är som medicin för kroppen. Det finns tillfällen men när du bara vill flytta genom din träning i minimal tid. Detta där en bra styrka och hjärtkrets kommer in i spel. Det är därför kretsträning är så effektiv, den kombinerar den perfekta blandningen av styrka och kondition på minimal tid. Slutresultatet är vanligtvis detsamma, en bra helkroppsträning och en kropp täckt av svett!<br/><br/>Det coola med kretsprogram är: de är snabba, roliga och mycket effektiva för att få resultat. Du blir aldrig uttråkad eftersom det finns så många designalternativ för en kretsrutin. Den presenterade styrkan och kardiokretsen kan nu hittas på Jefit-appen.<br/><br/>Vad är Circuit Training?<br/><br/>Circuit training är en träningsmetod som växlar mellan flera övningar (vanligtvis 4 till 12) som riktar sig mot olika muskelgrupper. En plan kan även använda olika rörelsemönster (som push, dra, tryck, bära, etc.). Utformningen av programmet gör det möjligt för någon att flytta från träning till träning med minimal eller ingen vila beroende på deras konditionsnivå. Några av de många fördelarna med regelbunden kretsträning inkluderar förbättringar i kroppssammansättning, muskelstyrka / uthållighet och aerob kapacitet.<br/><br/>Vad är intervallträning?<br/><br/>Intervallträning, å andra sidan, växlar mellan perioder av måttlig till högintensivt arbete med korta perioder av aktiv eller passiv vila. Den största skillnaden mellan kretsträning och intervallträning är inte vad du gör utan snarare intensiteten i det arbete som görs. Du har säkert hört talas om HIIT (högintensiv intervallträning) tidigare eller sett det i några av träningstitlarna på Jefit-appen.<br/><br/>Utrustningsbehov: Hopprep, viktplatta och hantlar<br/><br/>Kretsdesign: UB / LB / Kärna / Kardiosekvens<br/><br/>Kretsen session börjar med fyra övningar grupp tillsammans efter en kort uppvärmning. Sessionsdesignen växlar mellan en överkroppsövning, en underkroppsövning och en kärnövning innan du övergår till hjärt i 1-2 minuter. Programmet innehåller 20 övningar (inklusive fyra för uppvärmning) som består av fyra omgångar av "mini" kretsar med fyra övningar i varje grupp.<br/><br/>Så här ser var och en av de fyra kretsarna (efter uppvärmningen) ut i den senaste Jefit-kretsträningen:<br/><br/>Jag har personligen gjort kretsar i många år nu och, som många människor, arbetar genom en aldrig verkar bli gammal. I alla fall, kretsar är kul och få bra resultat, så varför inte ge en ett försök för din nästa träning?<br/><br/>Majoriteten av människor som utövar eller engagerar sig i enskilda eller lagsporter letar ofta efter sätt att förbättra prestanda. Med det, tar oss till hur vi bättre kan "hacka" vår kropp för att förbättra prestanda, vissa kallar också denna DIY-vetenskap.... biohacking. Dave Asprey, en biohacker som skapade företaget Bulletproof, definierar biohacking som "konsten och vetenskapen att förändra miljön runt dig och inuti dig så att du har full kontroll över din egen biologi."<br/><br/>Varför försöka hacka din kropp ändå?<br/><br/>Det finns många människor där ute som försöker hacka sin kropp för att förbättra prestanda, på någon nivå. De gör detta i grunden eftersom de har en stark önskan att må bättre och att se hur långt de kan driva människokroppen. Många människor hackar sin kropp i huvudsak för att försöka leva så länge som möjligt. Dave Asprey som ett exempel, har citerats som säger att han vill leva till 180 år gammal.<br/><br/>En annan välkänd kropp eller bio hacker är Tim Ferris, författare till den bästsäljande boken, 4-timmarskroppen. Ferris har ett välkänt rykte för att försöka hacka nästan allt som är relaterat till hans kropp. Varför gör han det? Börja läsa mat etiketter och hålla reda på din dagliga tillsatt socker. Sätt dig på en socker budget. Ät inte mer än 150 kalorier av tillsatt socker om dagen för män. Det är ca 38 gram per dag för män och 100 kalorier eller 25 gram per dag för kvinnor. Kolhydrater (socker) innehåller 4 kalorier per gram. Det finns två typer av sockerarter, naturligt socker och tillsatt socker. Tillsatt socker är gömt i nästan allt vi äter och dricker. Exempel på naturligt socker är mjölk och frukt, och till skillnad från tillsatt socker innehåller de mer fiber, vitaminer och mineraler. Detta kan vara en perfekt tid att börja tänka annorlunda om hur vi tränar hemma, vilket betyder hur vi strukturerar och utför vår träningsrutin. Förändring kan vara en mycket bra sak när det gäller en individs kropp och hur den kroppen anpassar sig och fortskrider under en träningscykel.<br/><br/>5 grundläggande mänskliga rörelsemönster<br/><br/>Oavsett om din träningsplan är inriktad på en helkropp, split rutin eller kretsträning, bör inte vara i fokus. Det som verkligen betyder mest är träningsrörelser inte muskler. Lita inte på specifika övningar, istället se till att du lägger till specifika rörelsemönster under varje träningspass. Rörelsemönster är träningsklassificeringar och kan ses som övningens rörelseriktning. Det finns olika skolor av tanke på hur dessa rörelsemönster kategoriseras och även talade om.<br/><br/>Följande är en modifierad version av några av de grundläggande mänskliga rörelsemönster som bör ingå i utformningen av något träningsprogram. Denna lista kan också innehålla andra kategorier som höft dominant, knä dominant, roterande och anti-rotational kategorier. För denna artikel kommer vi dock att hänvisa till följande fem grundläggande kategorier.<br/><br/>Squat<br/><br/>Varje variation av en Squat anses vara en av de bästa sammansatta rörelser du kan göra. Övningar som anges i denna kategori anses höft eller knä-dominant av vissa. Du kan tänka på denna kategori som övningar som använder muskler runt höft och knä, som en Barbell Squat, Dumbbell Squat eller en Goblet Squat, som exempel.<br/><br/>Hip gångjärn<br/><br/>Denna speciella kategori av övningar består av rörelser som innebär en "hängande" rörelse vid höftleden och har liten eller ingen rörelse vid knäet. Klassiska exempel på övningar som innehåller ett höftgångjärn är en Kettlebell Swing och en rumänsk Deadlift.<br/><br/>Höftgång Kategori: Kettlebell Swing träning<br/><br/>Dra: Vertikal & horisontell<br/><br/>Den vertikala dragningen inkluderar att flytta en vikt eller kroppsvikt vertikalt, i förhållande till den position som din kropp är i. Exempel på dessa typer övningar skulle omfatta Pull-ups och Lat Pull-down.<br/><br/>Den horisontella dragningen inkluderar alla övningar som flyttar vikten mot din kropp horisontellt. Några exempel är: böjda rader, inverterad rad eller en sittande rad.<br/><br/>Tryck: Vertikal & horisontell<br/><br/>Denna kategori kombinerar både vertikalt och horisontellt för att göra livet enklare för dig. Här listar vi varför du kanske känner på detta sätt och rekommenderar en lösning för att försöka få din gym motivation tillbaka.<br/><br/>Hur får du ditt gym motivation tillbaka<br/><br/>1. Få lite sömn<br/><br/>Sömnbrist kan faktiskt påverka oss mycket mer än folk tror. Om du har haft en dålig natt eller nätter, då det kan verkligen påverka din prestation, liksom din tankegång. Känner du dig trött och lider av brist på sömn kommer verkligen demotivera dig.<br/><br/>Försök att ställa in en sänggående rutin som du håller dig till. Gå och lägg dig 7-9 timmar innan du måste vakna så att du kan se till att du får tillräckligt med sömn. Även om du kan kämpa först, så småningom, kommer din kropp klocka komma ihåg och du kommer att kunna följa din sömn schema.<br/><br/>2. Ta några dagar ledigt<br/><br/>Ja, att spendera för mycket tid på gymmet kan vara en potentiell anledning till att du har förlorat motivationen. När du tränar, du ständigt sätta din kropp under stress. Det är därför vilodagar är viktiga-det är när din kropp återhämtar sig. Om du inte ger dig själv vilodagar, har din kropp inte tid att återhämta sig ordentligt. Förr eller senare börjar du känna det.<br/><br/>Så om du kommer till gymmet och inte känner för att träna, ta lite ledigt. En dag eller två, eller ännu mer hjälper dig att ta den tid du behöver för att återhämta dig. Se hur du känner efter att ha tagit lite gymtid.<br/><br/>3. Ändra din rutin<br/><br/>De flesta av oss tenderar att hålla sig till samma träning som vi vet. Vi håller oss till vad som är bekant och vad vi vet fungerar. Så småningom blir vi uttråkade. Om du känner dig omotiverad att gå på gymmet kan det bero på att din träningsrutin har blivit gammal och stillastående. Att göra samma träning om och om igen kommer inte längre att utmana dig. Utan stimulans förlorar vi vår motivation.<br/><br/>Prova att blanda ihop din träning. Jefit har massor av övningar som du kan välja mellan för att göra en ny träningsplan och hålla saker spännande. Gå med i en ny fitness klass som du inte har provat tidigare som boxning eller cykling. En omställning kan göra dig bra också.<br/><br/>4. Definiera ditt mål<br/><br/>Ett av de största misstagen som människor gör är att inte ha ett fitnessmål. Oavsett om det handlar om att gå ner i vikt, få muskler, slå en ny squat PR eller kunna springa en halv maraton på en viss tid, har något att arbeta mot den största skillnaden i gymmet.<br/><br/>5. Spåra dina framsteg<br/><br/>En annan anledning till att du kanske känner dig utbränd för gymmet är att du inte ser de framsteg du gör. Om du inte spårar dina framsteg, registrerar dina träningspass, tar framstegsbilder, kommer du inte att kunna se hur långt du har kommit. Det kan vara lätt att falla i rut av att tänka att du inte har gjort någon skillnad eller att saker och ting har förändrats. Därför är det viktigt att hålla koll.<br/><br/>Ingenting kan förnya din motivation att komma tillbaka till gymmet än att se var du var tidigare och var du är nu. Det kan hjälpa dig att föreställa dig var du kommer att vara i framtiden. Använd en journal eller en träningsapp (som Jefit!) som gör det enkelt för dig att logga allt och hjälpa till att få din gym motivation tillbaka.<br/><br/>6. Gör det till en vana<br/><br/>Detta kan låta kontraintuitivt men du behöver mer än motivation. Motivation är tillfällig och som du säkert vet, det kommer och går. För att se till att du fortsätter att gå till gymmet, även när du inte har någon motivation, måste du göra det till en vana.<br/><br/>Ställ in en träningsrutin som du håller dig till, oavsett om det går till gymmet före jobbet eller efter jobbet, eller till och med under dina lunchpauser. Det kan vara svårt att starta men om du håller upp det med tiden kommer det att bli en integrerad del av din rutin. Då kommer du att hitta dig själv att gå till gymmet även om du inte vill - för det är en vana. Det är ett bra sätt att få din gym motivation tillbaka.<br/><br/>Medan du kan göra ditt bästa för att undvika skador, tyvärr, de flesta människor kommer att uppleva dem då och då. Om du är skadad, då är det bäst att se en sjukgymnast eller professionell som kan behandla det ordentligt. Det finns dock några vanliga gymskador, och här är några lösningar för att behandla dem.<br/><br/>Hur man behandlar dessa vanliga Gym skador<br/><br/>1. Muskeldrag och stammar<br/><br/>Andra vanliga gym skador inkluderar dra eller anstränga din muskel. Detta är när en muskel är sönder eller översträckt. Även om detta kan inträffa var som helst på kroppen, är de vanligaste platserna anstränga din hamstring, eller nacke och rygg.<br/><br/>Det kan uppstå på grund av överansträngning, överansträngning och inte värma upp ordentligt.<br/><br/>Dra eller anstränga din muskel kommer att begränsa din rörlighet och kan orsaka smärta när du träffar en viss tröskel. Du kan också uppleva stelhet, svullnad eller svaghet. Det kan också variera från mild till svår.<br/><br/>Hur man behandlar det<br/><br/>Beroende på hur svår den är finns det olika sätt att behandla den. Vila den drabbade muskeln är av största vikt. Det kan vara frestande att "arbeta igenom smärtan", men detta kan göra det värre. Så ta ett par dagar innan du långsamt börjar införliva rörelse med muskeln. Tänk dock på att för mycket vila också kan orsaka styvhet så att du inte vill hålla den orörlig länge. Försök att hitta en bra balans.<br/><br/>När du börjar använda den igen, tryck inte för mycket. Överdriva det kan förvärra det.<br/><br/>Hur man förhindrar det<br/><br/>Uppvärmning är avgörande för att förhindra dessa vanliga gym skador. Du måste ordentligt förbereda din kropp för din träning i stället för att hoppa rakt in. Om dina muskler inte är varma, då riskerar du att riva den.<br/><br/>En bra uppvärmning bör vara specifik. Till exempel, om du planerar att huk, gör några luftsquats för att efterlikna samma rörelse som du kommer att göra, bara utan vikten. Det kan verka tråkigt men att ta sig tid att värma upp kan verkligen förbättra din atletiska prestanda, samtidigt som du förhindrar muskelstammar och drag.<br/><br/>Att lyssna på kroppen och veta skillnaden mellan smärta och god smärta är viktigt. Bra smärta är när du utmanar dig själv men inte går över din tröskel. Den dåliga smärtan som du inte vill ha är när du skadar dig själv till den punkt där du potentiellt kan dra eller spänna en muskel.<br/><br/>2. Löparens knä<br/><br/>En vanlig gym skada är Runner's Knee eller patellofemoral smärta syndrom. Det är när du känner smärta eller ömhet runt knäskålarna eller har problem med att sitta, stå, gå. Smärtan kan förvärras när du försöker gå nedåt också.<br/><br/>Det uppstår när knäskålen (patella) är feljusterad. Svaga eller snäva lårmuskler och överanvändning av knäet kan också orsaka det.<br/><br/>Trots namnet är löpare inte de enda som kan uppleva detta, även om det är vanligt bland dem eftersom löpning ställer stor efterfrågan på knäna. Alla andra övningar som kräver mycket användning av knäet kan orsaka det.<br/><br/>Hur man behandlar det<br/><br/>Om du känner smärta i och runt knäet då det första du kan göra är att vila det. Ta 3-4 dagar av träning. Om du tränar, sedan försöka undvika träning som innebär knät som lungor och huk.<br/><br/>En annan bra idé är att isa det. Icing det kommer att bidra till att minska svullnad.<br/><br/>Hur man förhindrar det<br/><br/>Hitta ett bra par skor som kan erbjuda riktigt bra stöd. Detta kommer att bidra till att minska efterfrågan på knäet så att du kan minska risken för att få löpar knä igen. Arch stöd kommer också att hjälpa till med detta också.<br/><br/>Inkorporera stärkande övningar i din träningsrutin för knäet. Arbeta på din underkropp som dina quads, nedre delen av ryggen, höfter och abs. Detta kan stärka områdena runt knäet och minska stressen som läggs på dem. Prova plank och glute broar.<br/><br/>3. Förstuvad handled<br/><br/>Handleden är ett lätt område att överbelasta och sätta för mycket tryck på. Eftersom det används i en mängd olika övningar och tar mycket vikt, är handleden belastning en vanlig gym skada.<br/><br/>Det kan vara svullnad och ömhet, och det gör ont att sätta press på handlederna.<br/><br/>Du kan få en stukad handled genom repetitiva rörelser som kan orsaka kronisk handled belastning. Å andra sidan är akut handled belastning när det uppstår plötsligt som böjer handleden förbi den normala tröskeln.<br/><br/>Hur man behandlar det<br/><br/>Ice handleden för att minska svullnad. Se också till att du vilar den. Att lägga till mer tryck på det kommer bara att göra det värre. Detta innebär att du bör undvika övningar som innebär att stressa på eller böja handleden.<br/><br/>Hur man förhindrar det<br/><br/>Om du är benägen att handleden stammar, sedan prova modifierade versioner av dina favorit övningar. Front squat genom att korsa armarna över bröstet istället för att använda handlederna. | According to Torah sources, the Hebrew year 6000 marks the latest time for the initiation of the Messianic Age.<br/><br/>משיח=קוראנא =358<br/><br/>There is a 164 year chronological discrepancy between the rabbinic dating for the destruction of the First Temple in and the academic dating<br/><br/>The discrepancy between the traditional and academic dating arises over the date of the destruction of the First Temple arising as a result of Jewish sages missing out the reign lengths of several Persian kings during the Persian Empire's rule over Israel.<br/><br/>Modern scholars tally ten Persian kings whose combined reigns total 208 years. By contrast, the ancient Jewish sages only mention four Persian kings totaling 52 years.<br/><br/>You should know, that the kingdom of Persia ruled 200 years during the time of the Temple and not 52 as Chazal have it. The names of the kings are as follows: Cyrus, his son Cambyses, Darius I, then Xerxes, the son of Darius of whom it is said that he is Achashverus, Artaxerxes I who ruled until 424 BCE, and so on for the kings of Persia until the year 334 BCE when Alexander of Macedon conquered them.]. Ezra's emigrated to the land of Israel in the time of King Artaxerxes which was some 80 years after King Cyrus of Persia's declaration.<br/><br/>This is even more bizarre, the sages say in Tractate Rosh Hashanah 3b that "Cyrus was Darius was Artaxerxes. Cyrus [Koresh] for he was a kosher king, Artaxerxes for the name of his kingdom, and his name was Darius." With this they turned three kings, who reigned over a period of more than 100 years, into one king! Don't wonder about it; in many places Chazal made several people into one. We will bring two examples. "Sheshbatzar is Danniel, and why was he called Sheshbatzar? For he withstood six (shesh) troubles (tzarot)" (Rashi on Ezra 1:8). About this did Ibn Ezra say (Daniel 1:4), "Daniel is not Sheshbatzar, with absolute proof." Similarly, "Keturah is Hagar," and the Rashbam wrote, "According to the plain meaning this is not Hagar" (Genesis 25:1).<br/><br/>The reigns of several Persian kings appear to be missing from the traditional calculations. !<br/><br/>It seems that for some reason 156 year of Persian rule over the Holyland the Sages did not include in the calander ?<br/><br/>The words<br/><br/>"seal the words and close the book"<br/><br/>In the book of Daniel as a positive commandment to obscure the calculations for the Messiah mentioned within, so that the true date of the Messiah's arrival would not be known<br/><br/>Ketubot 111a-b: Oaths That Bind<br/><br/>That the Jews should not return to the Land of Israel be-homah – "like a wall";<br/><br/>That the Jews should not rebel against the nations of the world;<br/><br/>That the nations of the world should not oppress the Jewish people overmuch.<br/><br/>Rashi interprets the first oath to mean that the Jewish people should not return to Israel by force, all together (in fact, some manuscripts have ka-homah, which would indicate "all together"). The Maharsha argues that it refers to the actual building of a wall – which would indicate a rebellion against the ruling nation. Such building could not be done without the permission of the nations that ruled over the land, as took place with the return of Ezra and Nehemia at the beginning of the Second Temple era.<br/><br/>Based on his reading of the pesukim in Shir HaShirim, Rabbi Levi believed that each passage implied a double oath. Aside from the three mentioned above, he adds three more:<br/><br/>That the end (ketz) should not be revealed;<br/><br/>That the end should not be pushed off;<br/><br/>That the secret should not be shared with the non-Jews.<br/><br/>Rashi understands that the fifth oath is a warning that sins and transgressions will delay the Messianic end of days, while the Maharsha suggests that it means that the people should not push off the ends of days in their hearts, but that they should believe that the Messiah will come swiftly.<br/><br/>There are also a wide variety of opinions regarding the secret that cannot be shared with non-Jews. While Rabbeinu Tam believes that it is the secret of the Jewish calendar and Rabbi Avraham Ya'akov Neimark in his Eshel Avraham suggests that it refers to the oaths that are under discussion, it may not be connected with a religious issue at all. In a mosaic floor that was found in Ein Gedi, a curse is placed on the person who reveals the secret of the city to non-Jews. It is an apparent reference to a security secret; a way to enter the city.<br/><br/>"They shall be taken to Babylonia and there they shall remain until the day that I recall them, said the Lord" (Jeremiah 27:22). Based on that verse, Rav Yehuda held that since the Babylonian exile was imposed by divine decree, permission to leave Babylonia for Eretz Yisrael could be granted only by God. The Gemara asks: And how does Rabbi Zeira interpret that verse? The Gemara answers that Rabbi Zeira maintains that that verse is written about the Temple service vessels, and it does not refer to the Jewish people, as the previous verse states: "Thus says the Lord of Hosts, the God of Israel, concerning the vessels that remain in the house of the Lord" (Jeremiah 27:21). Consequently, Rabbi Zeira sought to ascend to Eretz Yisrael.<br/><br/>The Gemara asks: And how does Rav Yehuda respond to this argument? The verse is clearly referring to the Temple vessels, not to the people. The Gemara answers that another verse is written: "I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, by the gazelles and by the hinds of the field, that you not awaken or stir up love, until it please" (Song of Songs 2:7). Rabbi Yehuda derived from here that no act of redemption should be performed until a time arrives when it pleases God to bring about the redemption.<br/><br/>And Rabbi Zeira maintains that the oath mentioned in that verse means that the Jews should not ascend to Eretz Yisrael as a wall, i.e., en masse, whereas individuals may immigrate as they wish. The Gemara asks: And what does Rav Yehuda reply to this? The Gemara answers that this command is derived from another verse in which "I adjure you" (Song of Songs 3:5) is written. The Gemara asks: And how does Rabbi Zeira explain the repetition of this oath in these verses?<br/><br/>The Gemara explains: That verse is necessary for that which was taught by Rabbi Yosei, son of Rabbi Ḥanina, who said: Why are these three oaths (Song of Songs 2:7, 3:5, 8:4) needed? One, so that the Jews should not ascend to Eretz Yisrael as a wall, but little by little. And another one, that the Holy One, Blessed be He, adjured the Jews that they should not rebel against the rule of the nations of the world. And the last one is that the Holy One, Blessed be He, adjured the nations of the world that they should not subjugate the Jews excessively.<br/><br/>And how does Rav Yehuda respond? It is written: "That you not awaken or stir up love" (Song of Songs 2:7), which serves to amplify and include a prohibition against Jews immigrating to Eretz Yisrael. The Gemara asks: And how does Rabbi Zeira explain the extra emphasis of this phrase?<br/><br/>The Gemara explains: He needs this phrase for that which was taught by Rabbi Levi, who said: These six oaths, i.e., the aforementioned three verses containing oaths, each of which contains the phrase "That you not awaken or stir up," why are they necessary? Three are those that we said and explained above. The other three oaths are as follows: That those who know should not reveal the end of days; and that they should not distance the end of days by saying that it is still distant; and that they should not reveal the secret of the Jews to the nations.<br/><br/>The Gemara discusses a phrase in the verse cited above. "I adjure you, O daughters of Jerusalem, by the gazelles and by the hinds of the field" (Song of Songs 2:7). Rabbi Elazar said: The Holy One, Blessed be He, said to the Jewish people: If you fulfill the oath, it is good, and if not, I will abandon your flesh and all will devour you like the gazelles and like the hinds of the field.<br/><br/>Rabbi Elazar said: Anyone who resides in Eretz Yisrael dwells without transgression, as it is stated: "And the inhabitant shall not say: I am sick; the people that dwell there shall be forgiven their iniquity" (Isaiah 33:24). Rava said to Rav Ashi: We learned this promise with regard to those who suffer from sickness. The phrase "I am sick" indicates that they are the ones who are forgiven their sins.<br/><br/>Rav Anan said: Anyone who is buried in Eretz Yisrael is considered as though he is buried beneath the altar. It is stated here: "An altar of earth [adama] you shall make for Me" (Exodus 20:21), and it is stated there: "For He does avenge the blood of His servants, and renders vengeance to His adversaries, and atones for the land of [admato] His people" (Deuteronomy 32:43). This teaches that one who is buried in the earth of Eretz Yisrael is considered as though he is buried beneath the altar in the Temple.<br/><br/>The Gemara relates: Ulla was accustomed to ascend to Eretz Yisrael from time to time. However, he died outside of Eretz Yisrael. They came and said to Rabbi Elazar that Ulla had passed away. He said: Woe for you Ulla, as through you a verse was fulfilled: "You shall die in an unclean land" (Amos 7:17). They said to him: But his coffin is coming for burial in Eretz Yisrael. He said to them: Even so, one who was absorbed by the soil of Eretz Yisrael while he was yet alive is not similar to one who was absorbed only after death.<br/><br/>The Gemara relates with regard to a certain man from Eretz Yisrael that a yevama, i.e., a woman whose childless husband died and left a surviving brother, happened before him, the surviving brother, for levirate marriage. This yevama was living in the district of Bei Ḥoza'a, far away in southeast Babylonia. The man came before Rabbi Ḥanina and said to him: What is the halakha as to whether I may descend to Babylonia to enter into levirate marriage with this woman?<br/><br/>א"ל אחיו נשא כותית ומת ברוך המקום שהרגו והוא ירד אחריו<br/><br/>Rabbi Ḥanina said to him: His brother married a Samaritan woman [kutit] and died. Rabbi Ḥanina described the man's late brother in these terms because he had left Eretz Yisrael to marry, and for the same reason he called his wife a Samaritan. Blessed be the Omnipresent who killed him. And yet the brother wishes to follow in his footsteps and descend after him? Better that he stay in Eretz Yisrael.<br/><br/>Rav Yehuda said that Shmuel said: Just as it is prohibited to leave Eretz Yisrael and go to Babylonia, so too, is it prohibited to leave Babylonia for any of the other lands. Rabba and Rav Yosef both say: Even to go from Pumbedita to Bei Kuvei, which is located beyond the border of Babylonia proper, is not permitted. The Gemara relates: A certain man left Pumbedita to live in Bei Kuvei, and Rav Yosef excommunicated him. A certain man left Pumbedita to live in Astonia, which also lay beyond the borders of Babylonia proper, and he died. Abaye said: Had this Torah scholar wanted, he would still be alive, as he could have stayed in Babylonia.<br/><br/>Rabba and Rav Yosef both say: With regard to the worthy of Babylonia, Eretz Yisrael absorbs them; with regard to the worthy of other lands, Babylonia absorbs them. The Gemara asks: With regard to what matter did they issue this statement? If we say that they were referring to matters of lineage, didn't the Master say: Lineage of residents of all lands are muddled compared to that of Eretz Yisrael, and lineage of residents of Eretz Yisrael is muddled compared to that of Babylonia. This means that the lineage of Babylonians was purer than that of the residents of Eretz Yisrael. Rather, they taught this with regard to matters of burial, i.e., the worthy of Babylonia are buried in Eretz Yisrael.<br/><br/>Rav Yehuda said: With regard to anyone who resides in Babylon, it is as though he is residing in Eretz Yisrael, as it is stated: "Ho Zion, escape, you who dwells with the daughter of Babylon" (Zechariah 2:11). This verse equates the two countries. Abaye said: We have a tradition that Babylonia will not see the pangs of the Messiah, i.e., it will be spared the suffering that will be prevalent at the time of his arrival. Abaye interpreted this statement in reference to the city of Hutzal deVinyamin in Babylonia, and as a result people call it Karna deShizavta, Horn of Salvation, as its residents will not endure the travails of the time of the Messiah.<br/><br/>Rabbi Elazar said: The dead of the lands outside of Eretz Yisrael will not come alive and be resurrected in the future, as it is stated: "And I will set glory [tzvi] in the land of the living" (Ezekiel 26:20). This teaches that with regard to a land which contains My desire [tzivyoni], its dead will come alive; however, with regard to a land which does not contain My desire, i.e., outside of Eretz Yisrael, its dead will not come alive.<br/><br/>Rabbi Abba bar Memel raised an objection from a different verse: "Your dead shall live; my dead bodies shall arise" (Isaiah 26:19). What, is it not the case that the phrase "Your dead shall live" is referring to the dead of Eretz Yisrael, whereas the subsequent phrase "My dead bodies shall arise" is referring to the dead of the lands outside of Eretz Yisrael? And if so, what is the meaning of the verse "And I will set glory [tzvi] in the land of the living"? This verse is written with regard to Nebuchadnezzar, as the Merciful One states: I will bring upon you a king who is as swift as a deer [tzvi].<br/><br/>Rabbi Elazar said to Rabbi Abba bar Memel: My teacher, I teach it from a different verse, as it is stated: "He gives breath to the people upon it, and spirit to they who walk there" (Isaiah 42:5). This indicates that the future resurrection is specifically for those who dwell in Eretz Yisrael. Rabbi Abba retorted: But isn't it written: "My dead bodies shall arise"? How do you interpret this verse? Rabbi Elazar replied: That verse is not referring to those living outside Eretz Yisrael; rather, it is written with regard to stillborns, as they too will merit resurrection.<br/><br/>The Gemara asks: And what does Rabbi Abba bar Memel do with this verse "He gives breath to the people upon it"? The Gemara answers: He requires that verse for that which was taught by Rabbi Abbahu. As Rabbi Abbahu said: Even a Canaanite maidservant in Eretz Yisrael is assured a place in the World-to-Come. It is written here: "To the people [la'am] upon it," and it is written there: "Abide you here with [im] the donkey" (Genesis 22:5). This verse in Genesis is traditionally interpreted to mean: A people [am] that is similar to a donkey, from which it may be inferred that even the members of this people merit a share in the world to come.<br/><br/>With regard to the aforementioned verse "And spirit to they who walk there" (Isaiah 42:5), Rabbi Yirmeya bar Abba said that Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Anyone who walks four cubits in Eretz Yisrael is assured of a place in the World-to-Come.<br/><br/>The Gemara asks: And according to the opinion of Rabbi Elazar, will the righteous outside of Eretz Yisrael not come alive at the time of the resurrection of the dead? Rabbi Ile'a said: They will be resurrected by means of rolling, i.e., they will roll until they reach Eretz Yisrael, where they will be brought back to life. Rabbi Abba Salla Rava strongly objects to this: Rolling is an ordeal that entails suffering for the righteous. Abaye said: Tunnels are prepared for them in the ground, through which they pass to Eretz Yisrael.<br/><br/>The verse states that Jacob commanded Joseph: "You shall carry me out of Egypt and bury me in their burying-place" (Genesis 47:30). Karna said: There are inner matters here, i.e., a secret meaning: Our Patriarch Jacob knew that he was completely righteous, and if the dead of the lands outside of Eretz Yisrael come alive, why did he trouble his sons to bring him to Eretz Yisrael? The reason is that he was concerned lest he not merit the tunnels.<br/><br/>On a similar note, you say: "And Joseph took an oath of the children of Israel, saying: God will surely remember you, and you shall carry up my bones from here" (Genesis 50:25). Rabbi Ḥanina said: There are inner matters here. Joseph knew concerning himself that he was completely righteous, and if the dead of the lands outside of Eretz Yisrael come alive, why did he trouble his brothers to carry his coffin four hundreds parasangs to Eretz Yisrael? The reason is that he was concerned lest he not merit the tunnels.<br/><br/>Rabba's brothers sent him a letter to Babylonia from Eretz Yisrael, in which they mentioned this idea that Jacob knew that he was completely righteous, as detailed above. They continued by writing that Ilfa adds matters to this statement: An incident occurred involving one who was suffering through his love for a certain woman he desired to marry, and he sought to descend from Eretz Yisrael. When he heard this idea concerning the tremendous significance of living in Eretz Yisrael, he suffered without leaving the country until the day he died.<br/><br/>Rabba's brothers further wrote in their letter: And although you are a great Sage, one who studies by himself is not similar to one who studies from his teacher, and therefore you should come to Eretz Yisrael. And if you say that you do not have a teacher in Eretz Yisrael, in fact you do have a teacher. And who is he? He is Rabbi Yoḥanan.<br/><br/>And if you do not ascend to Eretz Yisrael, be careful in three matters: Do not sit excessively, as sitting is harmful with regard to hemorrhoids; do not stand excessively, as standing is harmful with regard to heart trouble; and do not walk excessively, as walking is harmful with regard to eye problems. Rather, divide your time: One-third for sitting, one-third for standing, and one-third for walking.<br/><br/>Rabba's brothers offered him more advice in their letter: With regard to any sitting that is without support, i.e., an object on which to lean, standing is more comfortable than that position. The Gemara asks: Can it enter your mind that standing is better than sitting? Didn't you say that standing is harmful with regard to heart trouble? Rather, with regard to sitting<br/><br/>Its erev shabot. With a simple arithmetical calculation one can see that we are now in the period of Erev Shabbot afternoon. Just as every week has an erev Shabbos when we prepare for Shabbos, so does the world have its Erev Shabbot when the world prepares for it's own Shabbot. Just as the world was created in six days, with the seventh day being Shabbot. so this world exists for six thousand years, with the seventh thousandth year (called the seventh millennia) being the Shabbot of the world. With a simple arithmetical calculation one can see that we are now in the period of Erev Shabbot afternoon. The sixth day started in 1240 C.E. with the first 500 years being the night time period, since in Judaism the evening precedes the morning. The morning started in 1740 c.e. and noon time was 1990 C.E. Hashem therefore brought these emanations of the redemptive powers, amongst them liberty and equality, into the world. They remained for eight jubilees (8 x 50 = 400 years), that is to say they were in Jerusalem for 400 years. Exactly 400 years later, in 1917, the Ottoman Turks were conquered by the British. The League of Nations conferred the Mandate for the Holy Land and Jerusalem to the British. Thus, from 1917, under international law, Jerusalem was no-man's land.<br/><br/>Then, when Israel captured Jerusalem in the Six Day War of 1967, exactly one jubilee (50 years) after 1917, Jerusalem reverted to Jewish-Israeli ownership once again. Thereby, according to the prophecies of Judah Ben Samuel, the Messianic End Times began.<br/><br/>Ben Samuel was often called "Light of Israel." Even bishops came to him for advice. If anyone asked him where his wisdom came from he would answer, "The prophet Elijah, who will precede the Messiah, appeared to me and revealed many things to me and emphasized that the precondition for answered prayer is that it is fueled by enthusiasm and joy for the greatness and holiness of God."<br/><br/>The rabbi also prophesied that during the ninth Jubilee Jerusalem would be a "no-man's land." This is exactly what happened from 1917 to 1967, due to the fact that the Holy Land was placed under British Mandate in 1917 by the League of Nations and literally "belonged" to no nation.<br/><br/>Even after Israel's war of independence in 1948-49, Jerusalem was still divided by a strip of land running right through the heart of the city, with Jordan controlling the eastern part of the city and Israel controlling the western part of the city. That strip of land was considered and even called "no-man's land" by both the Israelis and the Jordanians.<br/><br/>It was not until the Six Day War in 1967 when the entire West Bank of the Holy Land was conquered by the Israeli army that the whole city of Jerusalem passed back into the possession of Israel. So once again the prophecy made by the rabbi 750 years previously was fulfilled to the letter.<br/><br/>It certainly would be significant if both 1917 and 1967 were Jubilee years, considering the significance of what happened in Jerusalem on those years. But it gets even more interesting, because Judah Ben Samuel also prophesied that during the 10th Jubilee Jerusalem would be under the control of the Jews and the Messianic "end times" would begin. Bortsett från de tre som nämns ovan, lägger han till tre mer:<br/><br/>att slutet (ketz) inte skulle uppenbaras;<br/><br/>att slutet inte skall skjutas upp;<br/><br/>Att hemligheten inte ska delas med icke-judar.<br/><br/>Rashi förstår att den femte eden är en varning om att synder och överträdelser kommer att fördröja den messianska slutet av dagar, medan Maharsha föreslår att det innebär att folket inte ska skjuta bort slutet av dagar i sina hjärtan, men att de ska tro att Messias kommer snabbt.<br/><br/>Det finns också en mängd olika åsikter om hemligheten som inte kan delas med icke-judar.
